steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content:
A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its graph just touches the x-axis at the vertex. For $y=-9x^2+30x+c$, the vertex occurs at:
$$x=\frac{-30}{2(-9)}=\frac{5}{3}$$

steps.title: Set the vertex height to zero
steps.content:
Substitute $x=\frac{5}{3}$ and set $y=0$:
$$-9\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^2+30\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)+c=0$$
$$-25+50+c=0$$
$$c=-25$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
